Q6.10: It's simply impossible to follow and not interesting at all
Q13.10: It's very challenging keeping the state of the play based on small snippets like this. If it could be presented with for example a minimap, possibly with numbers referenced in the text it would be easier to understand where the various events are taking place.
Q14.10: It's too boring to keep any kind of focus on it. Especially when it's not a match I care about. If this was between players I know or something like this I might be able to focus more.

Q6.10:
Same feedback - too much flavor text. Too difficult to actually understand the game itself versus a subjective interpretation of what's going on.

An example - "22:11 - Big action around the map! Near WoolyScout's Town Center, IcTiTaN's Elite Skirmishers and a Crossbowman were completely wiped out by a lone Knight and Builder defending, a massive loss for IcTiTaN! Simultaneously, WoolyScout's Crossbowmen are now aggressively raiding IcTiTaN's base, targeting his villagers! WoolyScout has also pushed out a Mangonel, while IcTiTaN is researching Leather Archer Armor."

What I would say: "22:11 - WoolyScout killed 12 skirmishers and 6 crossbowmen with 1 knight (side note - excerpt mentions a "Builder" but I can't see a villager contributing here). WoolyScout continued with army of 15 crossbowmen and killed 3 villagers in IcTiTaN's base."
Q7.10: For me the editorializing (e.g., "what a push!" "strong play!" etc.) do not add to and rather distract from how easy it is to read the play-by-play and truly understand what's going on. I'd rather just read "Player 1 lost 3 skirmishers, Player 2 lost 5 skirmishers in this battle" kind of thing. This would make it easier to mentally track what's going on in the match. Also an oddity about "adding man-at-arms or militia" (something like that) after a drush with man at arm upgrade had already been declared was confusing.
Q15.10: With every passing example I'm souring quickly to the editorializing which reads a lot like unnecessary AI summarization. Trying to mentally discard this information and get to the actual content of the game itself is quickly becoming annoying.
Q20.5: I don't think getting an experience close to actually watching a cast is possible with this medium. As a result I don't think the efforts toward introducing flavor text to tie events of the game together are worthwhile. I think the purpose of this type of summary should be to provide basic game updates for people who are able to read along but not watch. I don't benefit at all from being told what a big engagement!! every minor battle in a game is.

Q9.10: You put "Strongly agree" at the top as well as the bottom on the previous page, you silly goose
Q11.10: Awful choice of words, like an LLM that is too excited. Battles don't "erupt" in aoe2. Also, the impact of losses frequently seems to be rather exaggerated.
Q15.10:
There's still an eruption in there 😠
Also, many times, the commentary only tells us the outcome of battles, but not when a battle is happening

Q5.10:
AoE2 games are not meant to be read in blocks of text IMHO. Watching good casts is great, just listening to them in the background can even be nice, but reading about it lacks any of the energy and visual elements that make it fun. I wouldn't even read it if Tolkien himself wrote it 11.
When it comes to this particular text, I particularly dislike the fact it just feels like a transcript of a rather poor caster. Too many exclamation marks, not enough context of the map etc.
Q7.10: Again, this is not for me. Reading transcript-like text makes me reevaluate what I'm doing with my life.
Q16.10: This was better, but still it's just a slightly more eloquent take, using a medium that is just not right for experiencing an aoe2 game.
Q18.10: It's getting better, this was more concise while presenting some necessary details. But, again, reading about aoe2 games is not my cup of tea.
Q20.5:
I've mentioned it several times, but reading transcript-like text is terrible IMO. Even when it's about something I love like aoe2. When it comes from AI it makes it even worse.
If a person I like, like a funny caster or player, would write it, I might give it a go, but the written medium is just not it for experiencing aoe2.

Q11.10:
I couldn't get into it, I was lacking too much information to grasp enough what was happening to find it interesting. It's a strategy game, not a hype one, so I don't really care to learn that 3 skirms got killed, but rather how the battle changed or not the military balance, how much each player invested into it and how much it paid off, what's the status of the economies behind...
Also I'm struggling to replace it in a real game, like from the updates it seemed to me that they had two small fights in feudal age, but then it was already time to click castle age even though almost nothing had happened. And then during the game I felt like the Vietnamese player was dominating, but all of a sudden they lost for an obscure reason.
Q13.10:
In this one I think there's over-hyping at inappropriate moments (like when placing a new TC...). But again, I lacked a grasp of how the economies were comparing along the game, and what's the big picture looked like (e.g. when the Aztecs player lost their army in feudal, were they walled and on the way to castle age, ready to pike siege monk push, or fully opened and deep in feudal?).
I did like the hyping moments for the big fights; I think ideally there would be an alternation of hype moments (big battle, red lost everything) and strategical insights (blue adds a TC after finding damage with the push, securing the eco advantage).
Q17.10:
(obvious from my answers) I much more related to those match updates; I'd say the players' intentions were better comunicated, with the strategical context, and the relatable situation.
I'll just note that, regarding the questions about experiencing/improving from those match updates, I think there's a big layer of details that is (necessarily) missing from those updates but makes up the gaming (shape of woodline, where to quickwall, scouting pattern...) experience, hence why I don't think it'll work in that regard.
Loved the introduction at the beginning
Q18.10:
The few inconsistencies (e.g. crossbowmen before reaching castle age) realy threw me off. More significantly, it's way harder to get into the game without the visual, because well, that's how we know it.
I think the base descriptions (the gold being forward top-left) should be either more succint (jsut "forward", the strategical info) or if more precise, then the army movements should also be traced more precisely. Also it would help with the visualisation if we were given the base positions on the minimap (so that we know if "top-left" is forward or backward).
Q20.5: Personally I wouldn't be interested in getting a factual description of a game, nor get the strategical insights of an AI

Q4.10: The texts were way too long, if I want to read a book, I'll read a book. It depends on the use case, but, e.g. if this is somewhat to summarize matches on a timeline for YouTube timestamps, it's too much. It'd rather prefer to have a short summary. I believe, on its own, these texts in general don't work really well. They should be combined with other media.
Q7.10: Reading this is for me not the right medium. I'd rather turn on text-to-speech, so I don't need to scroll downwards reading, what would be way more interesting seeing live.
Q10.10: A bit too much text and filling words. But slightly better than the other texts before.
Q11.10: This is better, but I would still not read it instead of skipping through a video. I think they work well as an enhancement for A/V to find the right place to skip to, for example.
Q20.5: As said before, some texts are good. And I wouldn't read them all, but they can be useful to understand where to skip to in a video or on a stream. I would not sit down and read a whole match summary this way because it feels quite boring, and secondly it's not the real deal because in AoE2 competitive scene it's not just what someone does (what the summary focuses on), but how they do it, who does it and to experience with other players/viewers (chat systems). That's the fun aspect of it. These text can't replace that.

Q8.10: I feel like this was the best one of the 4, although there were still a few too many 'hype' moments ending in a ! that really shouldnt be.
Q10.10:
I know your e-mail adres is dutch, and being dutch too, i tried to come up with a better football analogy but i'm a baseball fan so here it is. 

I'm comparing this with the game logs that are provided of baseball games. To see an example, go to mlb.com, hover over any completed game and click 'wrap'. That will give the game log on the right hand side. You can read how someone flew out to center field, or how they got a base hit. But while the basic info is there, i don't think it comes close to actually watching and experiencing the game, or even highlights of the game. I had the same feeling with the match updates i just read.
Q12.10: This match update seemed more standardized, with all phrases ending with a !. I don't think that made it better to read, in fact the phrasing is somewhat worse.

Q4.10:
sometimes i felt like there was some info missing, for example, how did huns player get an early vil lead if there wasnt vils killed? idle tc from enemy?

maybe it needed a bit more of eco (vil count) updates overall, not just focused on military
Q12.10: this text felt a bit more messy, maybe because of the strat chosen by bulgarian player. but overall less descriptive than the first one

Q4.10:
I thought it was great that it identified what the map variance meant for the opening strategies of each player, however i wonder how much the forward gold was a factor, as it didnt examine other areas. I didnt get a good picture of the other resources, distance from each other etc. Also it would have been good to have had some extension of each of the players civs relationship to each other - so in addition to Britons: Strong archer civ Huns: Aggressive civ with no pop limit it would have been cool to have something like - Britons - their bonus to cheap tcs and archers suggests a defensive play but the huns have tarkans with high pierce armour which can counter archers (or words to that effect) 

Maybe something more about the players history with each other (i think thats available for comparison) 

For me, honestly i wouldnt read these for fun, enjoyment or learning - i would watch the visual streams - there is a lot more data being conveyed by this medium, however its great for when you have a tournament on and you cant commit to watching all the games - it works as a good summary of events.
Q9.10: This was the best of the three - necessary and sufficient information only. It had more detail but less content which is ideal.

Q10.10: Yeah so I think the sentencing is really off on certain places and if there is one thing that is really off-putting then it is the intense use of dramatic expressions. These match updates are like taking a transcript from MembTV (a very hyped up caster). This may work in a "live" environment, but absolutely not in text. Instead, I think it'd work much better if you keep it completely free of personal opinions and dramatic expressions. Would also be much better if you could condense more or less every single bullet point. Make it like sports update, basically.
Q14.10:
This one is significantly better than the previous one. It's way more compact and a lot of 'fluff' has been omitted. Still think a lot more could be removed though. For instance, I think you could question whether it's indeed useful information for a reader to know if a player's map is good or not without having FULL context at hand, and this would then of course just become way too long. 

Realistically, I think you could open:

Point 1: Player A as Briton against Player B as Franks.

Point 2: Player A opens 2 mil dr and Player B opens sc.

Point 3: Player A harasses Player B's berries, Player B cleans it with their scouts, taking x amount of losses.

Just try to write it as condensed as possible really.
Q16.10: Same as the first one really. It's like a transcript from a hype caster and it just doesn't work in paper form like this and even less so if it's live updates. I will refer back to the feedback provided on the second one.
Q20.5:
As I'm getting increasingly less time to follow friends and professionals who are active in the scene, it'd certainly be cool to have some live match updates when I'm unable to catch their games live. But the text has to be neutral and very short, just as sports updates are. 

If the AI is capable of understanding what is <<relevant>> information in a game and additionally is able to communicate it in a good manner, then you've got a very nice product^^

Q9.10: How was that game over in the middle of an ongoing tower rush?
Q13.10: Boring to read and filled with a lot of emotional nonsense that didn't fit the events, very annoying, no competence in filtering important information, at the same time lack of crucial basic information such as villager numbers.
Q14.10: I hate that I did this survey. How can I get back these 15 minutes of my life?
Q16.10: Tons of unnecessary information like concrete numbers of lost units or villager tasks while the key information is consistently missing.
Q20.5: The idea of an AI-based casting is cool, but with a quality like this, it's useless and annoying.

Q4.10: Units killed doesn't matter, it is the composition of the kills. If I have 20 xbows and 10 skirmishers in front, do I care if I lost the 10 skirmishers, they did their job as the meatshield.
Q5.10: Too much emphasis on Battle Events (I see what you are doing here, auto analyst), while completely ignoring the macro. Which player has upgrades, what is the eco like, is pressure on the gold villagers? Battle Events are not enough.
Q6.10: We need a eco indicator. Does one player have 20 villagers and the other 40? A villager kill is great but if you have not made a villager past 5 minutes then that one kill is not doing much.
Q7.10: Re-iterating my comment about Battle Events, they are not even half the game. Adding a 5 minute eco count would be helpful. Dude#1 has 30 villagers, 15 on farms, 10 on wood, 2 builders and 3 idle.
Q20.5:
AI reading only battle events is not enough. I guess this is similar to the SaltyEmpires which has an "ai caster" where it watches for events and zooms camera to it.

Post large battle, a unit counter is needed, including eco.

Q10.10:
The text was poorly written. also gave factually incorrect information. in arabia both sides start with 3 vils. not 5. and, regardless, expert players are going to start with 6 on food, not 5

Describing the starting gold and stone in the way that was chosen was, overly cumbersome. I would have gone with, both players have forward gold and stone, making it difficult to defend.. etc...

Q20.5: The updates have too much small detail without overall picture of the game

Q5.10: These players definitely feel higher level, though there's still some things that are weird (like the random unit transitions from red). The casting could do with some improvement though, there's no mention of possible strats (same for all the games so far), unit transitions are not foreshadowed at all (no mention of when the player starts adding a certain unit type, they just suddenly appear), and excessively using the player color + player name combined is unnecessary and a bit annoying. I also preferred when the positions of the player resources were better described, as that lets me better understand the map appearance (could be good to know the rough position on the map of players as well).
Q6.10: It's probably because I've read three of these now, but it's starting to feel less like these are proper casts, due to the lack of AoE style language. For example, Elite Skirmishers could often just be called "Skirms", Mangonels to "Mangos" etc. There's still a bit too much granularity in the precise number of units given (people don't really need to know that exactly 49 units died at the end for example). The players still don't read as pros, maybe 12-1400 elo. They were quite late to add any military, and made some weird choices (even by older game meta). There's also a lot of excessive army swings, but it does kind of make it more interesting in a way.
Q17.10: I still wouldn't consider these players pros by the sound of it, but it might also be the caster just missing a lot of info. CastleDropper going forward with vills should've been mentioned, as well as any forward buildings or generally the militia. I can follow along because I know how games like this go, but there was still a good bit of missing info (the militia line suddenly appeared when they mostly died), and we were not really informed as to the units being made by either player. Also would've been good to know where the towers were built in terms of resources.
Q19.10: Neither the players nor the caster read as very competent here. There's large gaps with absolutely no discussion going on, followed by a lot of noise about individual units dying and other excessively detailed information that generally isn't really very necessary. It sort of reads like a worse version of "Memb style casting", like it's trying to be hype, but gets bogged down too much with "IronBoar lost only 2 Crossbows but TC_Eater lost 23 vills". There are also some things that really stood out to me as the players not being very competent, namely: Losing three archers to a lumberjack, that should never happen, playing a lot of militia as Huns (especially going into Feudal), and TC_Eater not falling back after being late to Castle Age. There were also a few things that made the caster seem rather inexperienced, these being talking about Longbows just because IronBoar has started making archers (in a way that authoritatively states that Longbows will happen, when they likely won't), and the mistake with saying TC_Eater lost three Crossbows but then clicks up to Castle Age (implying they lost Castle Age units they wouldn't yet have access to).
Q20.5: I wasn't really thinking about any of them being AI generated, but in retrospect I would guess that they likely all are, on account of being overly specific in some ways (mentioning very precise unit counts), as well as missing any higher level discussion around strategy or gameplay here. If it was human written I'd be perhaps a bit more likely to, but generally if I wanted to engage with a competitive match I'd either play ranked, watch a top caster, or watch a pro game on YouTube.
